commit.message:
fix: retain agent references in Team, Workflow, Graph to prevent GC (#81)

Team.add(), Workflow.add_step(), and Graph.add_fork() passed agent.handle
(an integer) to Rust but did not store a reference to the Agent object.
Python's GC could free the Agent (and its native handle) while the
container still held a stale handle ID, causing 'Handle N not found'
errors at runtime.

Fix: store agent references in self._agents (Team/Workflow) or
self._nodes (Graph fork) so they live as long as the container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add RKNN inference script with CPU post-processing

New approach using community fork (zycer/yolo26_rknn_ultralytics):
- Model outputs 6 raw tensors (3 heads x reg/cls) without DFL/Sigmoid
- CPU post-processing: sigmoid + dist2bbox + per-class NMS
- Supports both ONNX (x86 verification) and RKNN (device) backends
- Verified: detects objects correctly with 6-tensor format

Root cause of previous INT8 detection failure:
DFL Softmax (2 ops) and class Sigmoid were inside the quantized model,
causing catastrophic precision loss under INT8. The fork removes these
from the model and handles them in CPU post-processing.
